pages.title: Our Purpose
pages.body:
Our Purpose

Bjerrehuus Fine Jewellery was founded on a single conviction: that truly beautiful jewellery is made slowly, with intention, and with full respect for the hands and earth involved in its creation.

We design for women who value permanence over trend. Pieces that stay with you for life, not just a season. Every piece begins as a real conversation and is not made until the design is exactly right.

Our studio is in the heart of Copenhagen. Our materials are sourced responsibly. Our standard is simple: if it is not exceptional, it does not leave the atelier.

pages.title: Production & Responsibility
pages.body:
Every Bjerrehuus piece is produced in partnership with certified workshops that share our commitment to ethical craft. We work exclusively with recycled and responsibly sourced precious metals, and our diamonds are sourced in accordance with the Kimberley Process.

We believe the most enduring jewellery carries a clean conscience. Each piece is made to be worn for a lifetime and passed on, which is why we hold every material and every maker to the same standard we hold ourselves.

If you have specific questions about the origin of a material used in a piece you are considering, please reach out to us directly at vibe@stylesnob.com.

pages.title: Diamonds: Nature & Lab
pages.body:
Diamonds

We select each diamond individually, prioritising cut above all other qualities. A well-cut diamond catches light in a way that colour grade alone cannot replicate.

Our standards

  • Cut: Excellent to Very Good
  • Colour: D–G (colourless to near-colourless)
  • Clarity: VS1–SI1, eye-clean
  • Certification: GIA or equivalent laboratory grading available on request

All diamonds used in Bjerrehuus pieces are conflict-free and sourced in compliance with the Kimberley Process Certification Scheme.

For bespoke commissions or questions about a specific stone, contact us at vibe@stylesnob.com.

pages.title: Customer Care
pages.body:
Customer Care

We are available Monday to Friday, 10:00–17:00 CET.

Email: client@bjerrehuusfinejewellery.com
WhatsApp: Available for existing clients and bespoke inquiries.

Response times

We aim to respond to all enquiries within one business day. For urgent matters, WhatsApp is the fastest route.

Bespoke & commissions

We welcome conversations about custom pieces — a resized heirloom, a bespoke engagement ring, or a one-of-a-kind gift. These conversations begin by email.

pages.title: Shipping & Delivery
pages.body:
Shipping & Delivery

Denmark

Free shipping on all orders. Delivery within 3–5 business days with tracking. Signature may be required on delivery.

International

International shipping is 299 DKK. Delivery times vary by destination and will be confirmed at checkout where available.

Customs and duties

International orders may be subject to customs duties and import taxes levied by the destination country. These charges are the responsibility of the recipient.

Packaging

Each piece arrives in a Bjerrehuus jewellery box with a polishing cloth and care card, presented in a sealed outer carton. All packaging is recyclable.

pages.title: Returns & Exchange
pages.body:
Returns & Exchange

Right of return

You may return unworn, unaltered items within 14 days of receipt in accordance with Danish consumer law. Items must be returned in their original packaging with all documentation.

How to return

Email us at vibe@stylesnob.com with your order number and reason for return. We will arrange a prepaid return label for Danish addresses.

Exchanges

We are happy to exchange a piece for a different size or metal colour where available. Contact us to check availability before returning.

Bespoke & custom orders

Custom-made and personalised pieces cannot be returned unless faulty.

Faulty items

If your piece arrives damaged or develops a fault within 24 months of purchase, we will repair or replace it at no cost. Please contact us with photographs of the fault.

pages.title: Jewellery Care & Repair
pages.body:
Jewellery Care

Fine jewellery rewards simple care. Follow these guidelines to keep your Bjerrehuus pieces in their best condition.

Daily wear

  • Remove jewellery before swimming, bathing, or using a sauna.
  • Apply perfume, lotions, and hairspray before putting on jewellery — not after.
  • Remove pieces before physical activity or manual work.

Cleaning

Clean with warm water and a few drops of mild, unscented soap. Use a soft toothbrush to gently clean around settings. Rinse thoroughly and pat dry with a lint-free cloth. Avoid ultrasonic cleaners for pieces with coloured gemstones.

Storage

Store each piece separately in the provided jewellery pouch or a fabric-lined box to prevent scratching. Keep chains fastened to prevent tangling.

Professional service

We recommend a professional clean and inspection every 12–18 months. Contact us at vibe@stylesnob.com to arrange servicing of your Bjerrehuus piece.

pages.title: Size Guides
pages.body:
Size Guides

Rings

We use international ring sizes. To find your size, measure the internal diameter of a ring that fits well, or wrap a strip of paper around your finger, mark where it meets, and measure the length in millimetres.

EU Size Diameter (mm) Circumference (mm)
50 15.9 50.0
52 16.6 52.0
54 17.2 54.0
56 17.8 56.0
58 18.5 58.0
60 19.1 60.0

Unsure of your size? We recommend visiting a local jeweller for a professional measurement, or contact us — we can advise on sizing for specific designs.

Bracelets

Measure your wrist with a soft tape measure or a strip of paper. Add 1–1.5 cm for a comfortable fit, or 0.5 cm for a closer fit. Our bracelet listings include the piece length in the product description.

Necklaces

Our necklace lengths are listed in centimetres in each product description. A 40 cm chain sits at the collarbone; 45 cm falls just below; 50 cm sits at the sternum.

Still unsure? Email us at vibe@stylesnob.com and we will help you choose.

pages.title: Meet Vibe
pages.body:
Vibe Bjerrehuus has spent over two decades in fashion and design production, working with her own brand and helping others bring their visions to life across Europe, Asia, and beyond.

In 2010, she co-founded a small jewellery workshop in Mumbai, India, with her Indian business partner. It has since become her second home.

After years of collaborating with high-end jewellery brands across the world, Vibe has turned her full attention to what has always mattered most to her: the pieces themselves. The gold, the diamonds, the gemstones. Materials that have carried meaning for centuries, and that she believes should carry meaning for generations to come.

Every Bjerrehuus piece is made with care for the people who wear it, the people who made it, and the world it comes from.

Her goal is not to produce jewellery at volume, but to create pieces that find the right person and stay with them.

pages.title: On gold, diamonds, and why I make jewellery
pages.body:
I started making jewellery because I could not find what I wanted to wear myself. That sounds simple, but it took years to understand what I actually meant by it. Not just the shape or the material — the feeling. Something that sits on your body every day and does not ask for attention, but is quietly, stubbornly there.

I work with gold and diamonds because they are honest materials. Gold does not corrode. A well-set diamond will still be catching light long after the person who wore it is gone. I spent time in Mumbai learning how pieces are actually made — how the prongs are set, how the surface is finished by hand. That process changed how I think about what a price means. You are not paying for a brand. You are paying for something real.

The stones I use are natural. I know that is a choice people question, and I think about it seriously. But I also believe in objects that accumulate meaning over time — pieces that get passed down because they meant something, not because they were new. A lab-grown stone is perfect. A natural stone has a history. I choose history.

I am sometimes asked why my pieces are expensive. The honest answer is that they are not, given what they are. 18K gold. VVS diamonds. Hand-finished in a workshop where I know the people doing the work. Hellere rigtigt end hurtigt — right rather than fast. That has always been the point.

pages.title: Terms of Service
pages.body:
1. Scope

These Terms of Service govern all purchases made through bjerrehuusfinejewellery.com, operated by Bjerrehuus Fine Jewellery (Copenhagen, Denmark). By placing an order you accept these terms in full.

2. Governing law

All agreements are governed by Danish law. Any consumer rights you hold under mandatory Danish or EU legislation are not affected by these terms.

3. Formation of contract

An order constitutes an offer to purchase. The contract is formed when we send a written order confirmation. We reserve the right to decline an order — for example if a piece is unavailable, if pricing has been listed incorrectly, or if we cannot verify payment.

4. Prices and VAT

All prices are shown in Danish kroner (DKK) and include 25% Danish VAT where applicable. Prices for orders shipped outside the EU are shown excl. VAT and may be subject to local import duties, which are the customer's responsibility.

5. Payment

We accept Shopify Payments (Visa, Mastercard, American Express), MobilePay, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and Shop Pay. Payment is captured at the time of order.

6. Delivery

Delivery times, carriers, and costs are described on our Shipping & Delivery page. Risk of loss passes to the customer upon delivery.

7. Right of withdrawal

Under the Danish Consumer Contracts Act you have the right to withdraw from your purchase within 14 days of receiving the goods, without giving reason. Made-to-order, engraved, or otherwise personalised pieces are exempt from the right of withdrawal. To exercise the right of withdrawal, contact us at client@bjerrehuusfinejewellery.com.

8. Returns and exchanges

Returns and exchanges are handled in accordance with our Returns & Exchange policy. Items must be returned unworn, in their original packaging, and accompanied by proof of purchase.

9. Warranty

All pieces are covered by a two-year warranty against material and manufacturing defects, in line with Danish consumer protection law. Wear and tear, accidental damage, and damage caused by improper care are not covered. Care guidance is set out on our Jewellery Care page.

10. Intellectual property

All content on this website — including photography, copy, designs, and the Bjerrehuus name and marks — is the property of Bjerrehuus Fine Jewellery and protected under Danish and international copyright law. No content may be reproduced without written permission.

11. Limitation of liability

To the extent permitted by Danish law, our liability is limited to the value of the goods purchased. We are not liable for indirect or consequential losses, including lost profits, lost time, or losses arising from third-party services.

12. Force majeure

We are not liable for delays or non-performance caused by events beyond our reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, strikes, transport disruption, or acts of public authorities.

13. Dispute resolution

If a dispute cannot be resolved between us, you may refer the matter to the Danish Consumer Complaints Board (naevneneshus.dk) or use the EU's online dispute resolution platform at ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr.

14. Contact

Bjerrehuus Fine Jewellery
Email: client@bjerrehuusfinejewellery.com
Telephone: +45 22 77 36 84

Last updated: May 2026.

pages.title: Your Privacy Choices
pages.body:
As described in our Privacy Policy, we collect personal information from your interactions with us and our website, including through cookies and similar technologies. We may also share this personal information with third parties, including advertising partners. We do this in order to show you ads on other websites that are more relevant to your interests and for other reasons outlined in our privacy policy.

Sharing of personal information for targeted advertising based on your interaction on different websites may be considered "sales", "sharing", or "targeted advertising" under certain U.S. state privacy laws. Depending on where you live, you may have the right to opt out of these activities. If you would like to exercise this opt-out right, please follow the instructions below.

If you visit our website with the Global Privacy Control opt-out preference signal enabled, depending on where you are, we will treat this as a request to opt-out of activity that may be considered a “sale” or “sharing” of personal information or other uses that may be considered targeted advertising for the device and browser you used to visit our website.
